name: Steve's MS-22
description: MS-20-inspired dual VCF in 4 HP. 12 dB low-pass feeds 6 dB high-pass in series. Link switch couples both cutoffs for bandpass. Modulation matrix routes one aux CV to HP, gain or resonance with bipolar polarity.

controls.name: FREQUENCY
controls.description: Low-pass cutoff. Covers sub to above audio range. Into self-oscillation when resonance is cranked, producing a sine at the cutoff.
controls.detail: 12 dB/oct LP · self-oscillates at high resonance · tracks 1V/oct via FCV

controls.name: SPREAD
controls.description: High-pass cutoff when Link=0%. In linked modes it becomes a bandwidth/offset control for the resulting bandpass response.
controls.detail: 6 dB/oct HP · offset in BP modes · bandwidth set by Link position

controls.name: RESONANCE
controls.description: Emphasis around LP cutoff. Pushed well past the MS-20 — from gentle to screaming self-oscillation. Thins bass when maxed.
controls.detail: mild to self-oscillation · past the MS-20's range · CV-able via matrix

controls.name: GAIN
controls.description: Input drive into the LP stage. Low values stay warm; high values push into asymmetric saturation (MS-20-style overdrive). Functions as a VCA when CV'd via matrix.
controls.detail: warm → asymmetrical drive · doubles as VCA when under aux CV

controls.name: FCV attenuator
controls.description: Attenuates the frequency CV input which tracks the LP cutoff. Set CCW for pure audio-rate listening; CW for full 1V/oct tracking.
controls.detail: ±5V → full range · calibrated for 1V/oct when full CW

controls.name: ACV attenuator
controls.description: Attenuates the auxiliary CV. Routed to HP/SPREAD, GAIN or RESONANCE via the matrix switches. Independent per destination.
controls.detail: ±5V range · shared by all matrix destinations

controls.name: LINK switch
controls.description: Three-way couples HP cutoff to LP cutoff. 0% = independent; 50% = wide bandpass; 100% = narrow bandpass tracking LP.
controls.detail: 0% · 50% · 100% coupling · SPREAD becomes BP offset in linked modes

controls.name: Modulation matrix switches
controls.description: Three-position switches assign ACV polarity per destination (HP / GAIN / RES). Off, +ACV or -ACV (inverted). Internal patch without cables.
controls.detail: per destination: +ACV · OFF · -ACV · three independent switches

inputs.name: IN
inputs.description: Audio input into the GAIN stage. Accepts Eurorack line levels; overdrive starts around halfway on GAIN.

inputs.name: FCV
inputs.description: Frequency CV input tracking the LP cutoff. Calibrated for 1V/oct when the attenuator is full CW.

inputs.name: ACV
inputs.description: Auxiliary CV input. Routed via the modulation matrix to any combination of HP/SPREAD, GAIN, RESONANCE with selectable polarity.

outputs.name: OUT
outputs.description: Main filter output, taken after the HP stage. Produces LP, HP or BP depending on Link switch and SPREAD settings.

behaviors.name: Serial LP→HP topology
behaviors.description: Audio passes through the 12 dB LP then the 6 dB HP. With Link=0% the two cutoffs are independent and you sculpt a bandpass by squeezing between them.

behaviors.name: Linked bandpass
behaviors.description: HP cutoff tracks LP cutoff at 50% or 100%. FREQUENCY sweeps both together; SPREAD offsets the HP relative to the LP, giving wide or narrow BP. FCV modulates the whole band.

behaviors.name: Resonance self-oscillation
behaviors.description: LP stage self-oscillates into a clean sine wave at cutoff. Track with FCV for a playable sine VCO. Thins bass as resonance rises — classic MS-20 behaviour.

behaviors.name: GAIN as VCA
behaviors.description: Route an envelope to GAIN via the matrix. GAIN becomes a CV-controlled input level / overdrive, turning the module into filter+VCA voice-card in 4 HP.

behaviors.name: Matrix polarity flip
behaviors.description: Inverts ACV at that destination. Envelope opens LP but simultaneously closes HP, or vice versa, for sweeps that move in opposite directions from one CV source.

behaviors.name: MS-20 drive character
behaviors.description: Discrete OTA core clips asymmetrically when driven. Adds even-order harmonics on transients. Pair with resonance for the characteristic MS-20 squelch.

patchIdeas.name: Classic MS-20 lead
patchIdeas.patch: Saw VCO into IN · FCV from envelope · RES past 3 o'clock · GAIN mid for bite · Link=0% for pure LP squelch
patchIdeas.diagram:
Steve's MS-22:
* Link: 0% · Res: 3 o'clock · Gain: noon

Envelope:

- VCO (Saw) -> Steve's MS-22 (IN)
- Envelope (Out) >> Steve's MS-22 (FCV)
- Steve's MS-22 (OUT) -> VCA (In)

patchIdeas.name: Self-oscillating sine voice
patchIdeas.patch: No input · RES maxed · LP self-oscillates · sequencer into FCV tracks 1V/oct · sine voice in 4 HP · add drive via GAIN for overtones
patchIdeas.diagram:
Steve's MS-22:
* Res: max · Link: 0% · FCV attn: full CW

Sequencer:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Steve's MS-22 (FCV)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- Steve's MS-22 (OUT) -> VCA (In)

patchIdeas.name: CV-swept bandpass wah
patchIdeas.patch: Link=100% · SPREAD sets narrow band · slow LFO into FCV sweeps the whole bandpass · guitar-through-eurorack wah
patchIdeas.diagram:
Steve's MS-22:
* Link: 100% · Spread: narrow

LFO:

- Guitar (Out) -> Steve's MS-22 (IN)
- LFO (Out) >> Steve's MS-22 (FCV)
- Steve's MS-22 (OUT) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Envelope → LP open / HP close
patchIdeas.patch: Matrix: HP=-ACV, GAIN=OFF, RES=OFF · envelope into ACV · same envelope into FCV · LP opens while HP closes → formant-like sweep with one EG
patchIdeas.diagram:
Steve's MS-22:
* Link: 0% · Matrix HP: -ACV

Envelope:

- VCO (Saw) -> Steve's MS-22 (IN)
- Envelope (Out) >> Steve's MS-22 (FCV)
- Envelope (Out) >> Steve's MS-22 (ACV)
- Steve's MS-22 (OUT) -> VCA (In)

patchIdeas.name: Filter + VCA voice card
patchIdeas.patch: Matrix: GAIN=+ACV · envelope → ACV · GAIN doubles as VCA · saves one VCA module for a full voice in 4 HP
patchIdeas.diagram:
Steve's MS-22:
* Matrix Gain: +ACV · Res: 2 o'clock

Envelope:

- VCO (Saw) -> Steve's MS-22 (IN)
- Envelope (Out) >> Steve's MS-22 (ACV) // acts as VCA CV
- Keyboard (Pitch) p> Steve's MS-22 (FCV)
- Steve's MS-22 (OUT) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Resonance accents
patchIdeas.patch: Matrix: RES=+ACV · accent trigger → envelope → ACV · resonance jumps on accented steps · drum-machine style squelchy bass
patchIdeas.diagram:
Steve's MS-22:
* Matrix Res: +ACV · base Res: 1 o'clock

Accent EG:

Sequencer:

- Sub VCO (Saw) -> Steve's MS-22 (IN)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Steve's MS-22 (FCV)
- Accent EG (Out) >> Steve's MS-22 (ACV)
- Steve's MS-22 (OUT) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Audio-rate screamer
patchIdeas.patch: Audio-rate sine into FCV · GAIN high · RES high · FM screams and drive clip mix for metallic industrial tones · Link=50% for spectral twist
patchIdeas.diagram:
Steve's MS-22:
* Gain: 3 o'clock · Res: max · Link: 50%

Modulator VCO:

- Carrier VCO (Saw) -> Steve's MS-22 (IN)
- Modulator VCO (Sine) >> Steve's MS-22 (FCV)
- Steve's MS-22 (OUT) -> VCA (In)

patchIdeas.name: Stereo split via matrix
patchIdeas.patch: Two identical patches through one MS-22 isn't stereo — but flipping matrix polarities (+/-ACV on HP vs RES) from one shared CV creates wide spectral movement · pan the result by patching dry IN to left and MS-22 OUT to right
patchIdeas.diagram:
Steve's MS-22:
* Matrix HP: +ACV · Matrix Res: -ACV

VCO:

LFO:

Mult:

- VCO (Saw) -> Mult (In)
- Mult (Out 1) -> Steve's MS-22 (IN)
- Mult (Out 2) -> Output (L)
- LFO (Out) >> Steve's MS-22 (ACV)
- Steve's MS-22 (OUT) -> Output (R)
